message:
test(raptorq): add markdown-vs-artifact status drift detection for prerequisite snapshot tables

The prerequisite status snapshot tables in the baseline bench profile and
unit test matrix documentation can silently drift from the canonical
correctness_prerequisites array in the G1 budget draft JSON artifact.
When a bead status is updated in the artifact but the corresponding
markdown table row is not, downstream human readers see stale information.

Adds two cross-validation tests and a shared helper:

- markdown_status_for_bead(): parses a markdown table to extract the
  status cell for a given bead ID
- g1_budget_baseline_markdown_status_snapshot_matches_artifact: iterates
  all correctness_prerequisites and asserts each bead's status in the
  baseline bench profile markdown matches the artifact JSON
- g1_budget_unit_matrix_markdown_status_snapshot_matches_artifact_subset:
  same validation for the unit test matrix markdown, restricted to the
  D5/D6/D7/D9 bead subset that appears in that document

Both tests use include_str! to compile the markdown docs into the test
binary, ensuring drift is caught at compile-time test rather than
requiring runtime file access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
